title: Display Notches
description: Adds a new alternative to Item Frames that offers higher customisability and functionality.
body:
Item Frames in Minecraft are great for what they are, and the game itself would be worse off without them. At the same time, they have quite a few aspects that really hold them back from achieving their potential.

This is where Display Notches come in! They represent a reimagined version of Item Frames, that solve many of their shortcomings, while still leaving a couple niche uses, as to not entirely undermine them.

**Below is a detailed description of what Display Notches can do and at the bottom of it, a short summary of Pros and Cons.**



# How To Obtain

Display Notches can be crafted from one Item Frame (or Glow Item Frame) and one Blackstone, in any orientation.

<center>
  
![Display Notch Crafting Method using one Blackstone and one Item Frame.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/38a9bffa87a57d55fcf6510a6a834af5655dba3c.png)
</center>

# How To Use 
- They can be placed on all six sides of a block and can be right-clicked with an Item to insert it. Right-clicking with an empty hand or another item will then take out its contents. The direction the Display Notch is facing will affect the item's display as well.

<center>
  
![Display Notch placed on all faces of a block.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/99988af8fa60491e02c288967ea5b4dd7bdfd064.png)
</center>

- The size of 2D displayed items are set to match the pixel sizes of a block. That means that any item that is 16x16 pixels will fully cover the face of a block (See Red Glass Pane below as an example).

<center>
  
![2D Items shown](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c0ea1af8a3e013d52d2010609087483051ffe70c.png)
</center>

- When it comes to block-items, they are sized at a one-eight (1:8) scale.

<center>
  
![Block Items shown](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ba68b105dfa2daaefccf45b366e010a8aae198e0.png)
</center>

- Display Notches can be waterlogged and even lavalogged.
  
<center>

![Water and Lava-logged Notches shown](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8a65996c1ede940b26128ee99dbb21b7a2d7df78.png)
</center>

- Once an Item has been inserted, then it becomes possible to manipulate how it is displayed (NOTE: An empty Display Notch cannot be manipulated, but also, taking an item out and placing a new one in will not delete any prior changes made). There are 7(+2) ways to do so: Right-Clicking with an Axe, a Shovel, a Pickaxe, a Glow Ink Sac, Dyes, (Phantom Membrane, Tripwire Hook) or some Glass; and using a Redstone Signal. None of these interactions use durability or consume the item.
  
- Right-clicking with an Axe will change between the item laying down flat or standing up.

<center>

![Display Notches Axe Interaction Showcased, with the items being flat and standing.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1dc0789308c9432f12442d2e1ac27edaf6e0122a.png)
</center>

- Right-clicking with a Shovel sinks or elevates the item.

<center>

![Display Notches Shovel Interaction Showcased, with the items being raised and sank.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/467cec74855be2b24195948ba17aa30b9c071c85.png)
</center>

- Right-clicking with a Pickaxe, the rotation of the item can be changed to 16 different options (same as an Item Frame). If the item is standing up, the axis of rotation will also be changed. The rotation can also be read with a Comparator and outputted as a redstone signal.

<center>

![Display Notch Pickaxe Interaction Showcased](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/58c16e0465d110e0eefb8624cad21b5d6bd1db7c.png)
</center>

- Right-clicking with a Glow Ink Sac will make the displayed item glow. This is a visual change, the Display Notch will not actually give off light.

<center>

![Display Notches Glow Ink Sac Interaction Showcased](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7eb190597831c26400bcfa2863a7d11f851c0312.png)
</center>

- Right-clicking with a Dye will change the Display Notch itself to the corresponding color. It does not have any effect on the displayed item.
  
<center>

![Died Display Notches showcased in all 16 colors](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9733b4f8267d759bd64e43acdba6c9f294e7de76.png)
</center>

- (As of version 1.0.1) Right-clicking with a Phantom Membrane will make it so the Display Notch no longer has a solid collision.

<center>

![Two Creepers, one standing inside a Display Notch (in Phantom state) the other standing on top of one.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/5dbc6dfb44e06943f9b0782d5724c8d7029629aa.png)
</center>

- Right-clicking with a Glass block will turn the Display Notch itself invisible. It does not have any effect on the displayed item.

<center>

![Invisible Display Notch showcased](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/5c39456a9fe3a70cc01c082e89e9f7f459f88193.png)
</center>

- It might be difficult to find an Invisible Display Notch once the item has been removed. To solve this, you can simply hold a Display Notch in your hand, and that will mark all invisibles ones around you.

<center>

![Green Excalamtion Marks showing where Invisible Display Notches are](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/78274e702a04f2cac17b9e7541d23d5d2ae387f8.png)
</center>

- (As of version 1.0.2) Right-clicking with a Tripwire Hook will lock the Display Notch, stopping any changes until broken or unlocked again with the Tripwire Hook. Redstone behaviours are not affected by this.

- Display Notches will react to being powered by a Redstone Signal, and will start automatically rotating the displayed item.
  

- Lastly, Display Notches can interact with Hoppers and Droppers (though modded pipes and such will not work in most cases).


# Pros and Cons Compared To Item Frames
## Pros
- More customizability when it comes to how the item is displayed
- More options for how the displaying medium looks
- Items are better scaled to the environment
- Generally better performance (as they are non-ticking block entities)
- Aren't affected by entity damaging factors
- Optional solid hitbox, making custom platforms, stairs etc. possible
- No need for block support (they will not disappear if the block they are attached to is removed)
- Interaction with redstone, hoppers and droppers
## Cons
- You can only have one Display Notch within the same block, compared to the possibe 6 with Item Frames
- No interaction with projectiles or entities (displayed item won't pop out)
- Maps will not display their contents



# Credits
